column4: Fixing Critical Failures in Spot Pricing Workflow Leading to Operational Inefficiencies and IQ issues
column8:
Unsuccessful Price Task Closures: Frequent failures due to system errors, unsupported logic, and technical limitations are preventing timely and accurate pricing task completion.

Surcharge Drop Issue: Surcharges initially applied during booking are being dropped at later stages in specific scenarios (acknowledhed by penny platform), leading to incorrect rate instances and revenue leakage.

Lack of Support for Commodity-Based Surcharge Exemptions: The pricing engine for Spot and Maersk Go does not accommodate local regulatory exemptions (e.g., INE not applicable for Cashew, Coffee, Cocoa, Rubber in Guinea; port surcharges exempted for essential goods in Cameroon). This results in manual post-booking adjustments (Manual efforts) , IQ impact, and customer disputes.

Incorrect Repricing After Inland Changes: When inland locations are added, removed, or changed on existing Spot bookings—especially after a carrier-initiated roll—the system often triggers incorrect repricing, leading to incorrect invoices and disputes

column4:
Amendment Improvement:
(1) Amendment triggered either through customer (SSIB & SSDO), Agent (GCSS) or dispatcher (from TMS) should be with amendment reasons 
(2) Enable selection of Customs stop, staging location etc in the export inland leg after  containers are picked up from customer facility. Amend the existing shipment, trigger haulage order to TMS. Amendment should be aligned with container execution status from TMS. Customisable at country level. 
(3) Enable performance management to identify CAD / OTA improvement basis Customer Appointment Date amendment.
(4) Introduce customer appointment date validation at the time of booking to restrict customer from feeding dummy dates for inland transportation. This could vary per country.
(5) Auto haulage trigger from GCSS to TMS to enable instant confirmation to customer

column4:
AFRI system validation to be brought for D&D
To create visibility of freetime & payment status for D&D charge in GCSS
For Cash customers, 40–95% of D&D billing remains outstanding month-on-month and consistently migrates into long-ageing buckets, while Freight outstanding remains controlled. The root cause is the absence of payment enforcement at empty return, making D&D a post-service, unsecured receivable.

column4:
Unsuccessful Price Task Closures: Frequent failures due to system errors, unsupported logic, and technical limitations are preventing timely and accurate pricing task completion.

Surcharge Drop Issue: Surcharges initially applied during booking are being dropped at later stages in specific scenarios (acknowledhed by penny platform), leading to incorrect rate instances and revenue leakage.

Lack of Support for Commodity-Based Surcharge Exemptions: The pricing engine for Spot and Maersk Go does not accommodate local regulatory exemptions (e.g., INE not applicable for Cashew, Coffee, Cocoa, Rubber in Guinea; port surcharges exempted for essential goods in Cameroon). This results in manual post-booking adjustments (Manual efforts) , IQ impact, and customer disputes.

Incorrect Repricing After Inland Changes: When inland locations are added, removed, or changed on existing Spot bookings— after a carrier-initiated roll—the system often triggers incorrect repricing, leading to incorrect invoices and disputes
SSIB VAS - for mixed container size/type

column4:
Configurable Event Delivery Layer for Ocean Track & Trace API

Triggered by Walmart integration (APIS-4918), this initiative builds a platform-level capability rather than a one-off customization. Three core features:

1. Configurable payload format - option to strip Cloud Event envelope and deliver raw event arrays
2. Container (EQ) references on transport events - link containers to vessel movements
3. Incremental (latest-only) event delivery - shift from full transport plan snapshots to real-time deltas

This enables leaner integrations, reduces consumer-side deduplication effort, and aligns with DCSA V2 evolution.
column8:
• API premium tier adoption rate (target: 15+ customers in Year 1)
• Incremental revenue from real-time tier ($)
• Walmart integration go-live (target: Q2 FY next year)
• Event delivery latency (target: <5 min from source event)
• Data quality score on incremental events (target: >99.5% accuracy)
• Customer NPS / CSAT on Track & Trace API

Value
• Premium tier pricing for real-time incremental feed (est. $50 to 100k)
• Reduced churn risk on Tier-1 accounts (Walmart)
• Marginal cost low once platform is built - high-margin revenue stream
column10:
High - Tier-1 Customer Retention & Acquisition

• Walmart: unblocks integration currently stalled on APIS-4918
• Tech-first customers (retailers, 3PLs, freight forwarders) increasingly demand lean, real-time APIs
• Competitive parity: major carriers already offer incremental event delivery
• Potential to convert existing batch consumers to premium tier
column11:
Significant - Simplified Integration

• No more deduplication logic on consumer side
• Smaller payloads reduce bandwidth and processing time
• Container-level granularity enables precise operational workflows
• Real-time events enable downstream automation (ETA alerts, exception handling)
